justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and two of six vehicles, missing the majority of actors and thus largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected actors the extraction provides useful attributes such as actions, context, age, type and door/trunk status, but it omits many actors and some ground truth attributes and uncertainties.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but they are sparse, partially ambiguous, and not comprehensive across all actors in the scene.
justifications.overall: The extraction gives moderately rich semantics for a few detected objects but misses most actors and many spatial details, resulting in limited overall usefulness for scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian instead of two and only two vehicles while the ground truth lists multiple additional vehicles and differing motion states, so many instances are missing or misassigned.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected entities the extraction provides several semantic attributes (atomic action, age, vehicle type, trunk and door status and some context), but it omits numerous objects and contains incorrect motion labels compared to the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The output includes plausible spatial relations for the few detected objects, but these relations are incomplete overall and the stated motion/position of the white truck contradicts the ground truth motion status.
justifications.overall: The extraction is descriptive for the objects it detects but is substantially incomplete and contains important motion and instance errors, reducing its overall utility.

justifications.completeness: The VLM detected only one pedestrian and two vehicles while the ground truth lists four persons and five vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains useful attributes (actions, age, motion status, doors/trunk) but omits object identifiers, counts, many objects, and richer contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are provided but they are sparse, somewhat vague, and potentially misleading given multiple pedestrians and vehicles in the scene.
justifications.overall: Due to significant omissions of objects despite moderate attribute detail, the extraction is of limited utility for robust driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and two of four vehicles (3 of 6 total annotated objects), missing multiple vehicles and a person.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attributes per detected entity (actions, age, motion status, trunk/doors, communicative/transporting) but lacks track IDs and omits many objects, with potential motion-status mismatches.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers plausible but coarse spatial relations for reported objects (e.g., 'between buildings', 'near storefront'), yet relations are vague, not tied to GT object IDs, and partly unverifiable.
justifications.overall: Moderately useful extraction with good semantic detail for detected items but hampered by incomplete detection, vague spatial ties, and some attribute uncertainties.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses all three pedestrians and reports only two of five vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: It provides vehicle types, door and trunk states, and a logo description, but omits actor actions and many entities, limiting overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are given and may be partially correct, but they are sparse and some motion labels conflict with ground truth.
justifications.overall: Missing pedestrians, under-reporting of vehicles, and incorrect or incomplete motion and spatial information result in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Many objects are missing or unrecorded compared to ground truth with only one of four pedestrians and three of six vehicles detected and several attributes omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: Several important semantic attributes are incorrect or absent such as pedestrian actions and carrying status, and vehicle motion labels are partially correct but incomplete.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are coarse and partially correct for a few objects but many objects lack any spatial relation and some relations are ambiguous.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few high level items but is incomplete and has multiple semantic and spatial omissions or errors, yielding limited utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three vehicles and no pedestrians while ground truth lists three persons and six vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided types, motion status, and trunk/door info for detected vehicles as well as spatial hints, but omitted pedestrian attributes and several vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the three detected vehicles are plausible and informative but relations for the missing pedestrians and parked vehicles are absent.
justifications.overall: Extraction has accurate per-vehicle semantics for detected objects but misses all pedestrians and several vehicles, reducing overall utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and three vehicles while the ground truth contains six pedestrians and seven vehicles, so it captures only a small fraction of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes are provided (age, motion status, simple type) and a few motion labels match ground truth, but many behavioral and contextual attributes are missing or incorrect for pedestrians.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few coarse spatial relations are stated for reported objects, but most scene-relative placements and relations for the many missing objects are absent or underspecified.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers a limited and partially correct account of a few objects but misses many entities and important semantic and spatial details, yielding overall low reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing multiple objects and many required annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes action, basic context, and vehicle attributes but lacks numerous person-level attributes and richer contextual labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides some spatial relations for reported entities but omits relations for most ground truth objects and uses vague descriptions.
justifications.overall: Partial and shallow extraction with significant omissions and incomplete coverage leading to low overall utility for downstream perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected one pedestrian and one vehicle while ground truth contains seven pedestrians and five vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like walking and adult but misses most actors, several contextual labels (e.g., carrying, talking) and many vehicle motion distinctions, so semantic detail is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial claims such as pedestrian at a pedestrian crossing and parked near a store are unsupported or incorrect relative to the ground truth positions and most spatial relations are missing.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially inaccurate, missing most objects and many key attributes, resulting in poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction reports one pedestrian and two vehicles while ground truth contains seven pedestrians and four vehicles, so many entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes some attributes like age and actions but mislabels vehicle type and motion for several vehicles and omits many person-level attributes such as transporting and communicative for most individuals.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but they are sparse and partially incorrect compared to ground truth contexts, for example labeling a pedestrian as using a crosswalk when the ground truth indicates jaywalking and carrying.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a few correct attributes but misses most entities and contains several incorrect relations, yielding poor to moderate performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles, missing the majority of objects present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes multiple attribute fields for the detected entities (actions, contexts, age, trunk/door status) but many entity-specific attributes and most entities are not reported.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides some spatial descriptors but the pedestrian spatial relation conflicts with ground truth contexts and most objects lack spatial relations.
justifications.overall: Low object recall and partial, sometimes inaccurate attributes result in overall poor alignment with the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 of 5 ground truth pedestrians and 1 of 5 ground truth vehicles are reported, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple attribute fields for reported entities but omits some ground truth labels (for example a communicative action) and lacks entries for several objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are given but several pedestrians that are on the road in the ground truth were labeled as on sidewalks and vehicle placement is described only generically.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains useful attribute information for the few detected objects but is substantially incomplete and has notable spatial labeling errors.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and 1 of 5 ground-truth vehicles, missing most objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful attributes (atomic actions, age, simple context) for the detected entities but omits communicative detail for one ground-truth person and many objects are entirely missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers plausible local spatial relations for the reported entities, but several ground-truth objects have no spatial tags and the relations cannot be fully verified against the annotations.
justifications.overall: Partially informative for the detected instances with good attribute detail, but low recall of pedestrians and vehicles reduces the overall utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only 2 of 6 pedestrians and 1 of 5 vehicles from the ground truth, missing the majority of objects and their annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the extraction includes several attribute fields (atomic action, context, age, door/trunk status), it omits many object instances and fails to capture specific labels such as jaywalking and a communicative group for one pedestrian.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are generic and partially incorrect relative to ground truth contexts (several pedestrians labeled as walking along the side when many were on the road or jaywalking).
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction provides some useful attribute-level information but is incomplete and contains spatial/contextual inaccuracies that limit its reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles from the ground-truth and omitted multiple pedestrians and their specific attributes like carrying or communicative actions.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided basic labels (atomic action, age, parked/moving) but missed several detailed annotations in the ground-truth such as carrying, talking on phone, jaywalking and additional pedestrians, reducing semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Included some spatial cues (sidewalk, near store, parked in front) but also mislocated pedestrians that ground-truth marks as walking on the road and lacked precise relations for several objects.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some correct entities and basic attributes but is incomplete and misses important behaviors and spatial placements, yielding limited reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only 3 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing several actors and important motion states so overall coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes many semantic attributes (actions, age, transporting, communicative) but several labels are incorrect or absent compared to ground truth, reducing usefulness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are provided (on the road, near building) but the extraction misses critical context like jaywalking and misplaces others, limiting spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Due to substantial omissions of actors and several incorrect or missing semantic and spatial labels, the extraction is only partially reliable for driving-scene perception.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth entities are missing or mismatched (only 3 pedestrians vs 4 GT, 3 vehicles vs 5 GT) and several vehicle motion states are incorrectly reported as parked.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple attribute fields (age, communicative, trunk_open, doors_open) but often leaves them as 'none of the above' or assigns incorrect actions/types (e.g., biking labeled under pedestrian with inconsistent atomic_action).
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are given, but they are imprecise or inconsistent with GT (sidewalk vs road distinctions and relative motion/positions for vehicles are missing or wrong).
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic scene elements and some attributes but misses several objects, mislabels actions/motion states, and provides only partially accurate spatial and contextual details.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians including a false bicyclist and two of six vehicles, so overall recall of actors is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Many attribute fields are present but several attributes are incorrect or missing (eg communicative actions) and multiple actors are omitted or misclassified.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides plausible spatial relations for the reported detections, but coverage is limited and not grounded to all ground truth objects.
justifications.overall: The output offers some useful attribute and spatial information but low actor recall, a critical misclassification, and missing communicative context reduce its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected only half of the pedestrians and one of four vehicles, missing several pedestrians including jaywalkers and moving vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields but contains incorrect or missing labels (missed group communication and jaywalking, wrongly marked a person as carrying) and inconsistent vehicle typing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial tags but several are inconsistent with ground truth (many pedestrians labeled along sidewalk while GT indicates walking on the road or jaywalking), though vehicle left-side placement is correct.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially useful but incomplete and contains multiple semantic and spatial errors, so it has limited reliability for safety-critical perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians and no vehicles, missing multiple ground-truth persons and all vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted some attributes (age, atomic action) but missed key contextual labels like jaywalking and vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided local shop-based relations for two people and one correct side-of-road context, but failed to capture jaywalking spatial context and vehicle locations.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few correct person attributes but omits several actors and important context, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only reports 2 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while the ground truth contains 4 people and 4 vehicles, missing a majority of entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes like atomic actions and age are provided, but key contextual labels such as jaywalking and multiple vehicle motion states are missing or incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present for listed actors and one vehicle, but several actors lack any spatial info and some placements are imprecise relative to ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some useful semantic details but misses many entities and important context, yielding a below average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only half of the pedestrians and one of three vehicles, missing several jaywalking pedestrians and moving cars.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, age, communicative) but mislabels contexts (misses jaywalking and moving vehicle states), limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial tags are generic and often incorrect (labels pedestrians on the sidewalk whereas ground truth shows multiple people on the road/jaywalking).
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some attributes but misses many actors and misrepresents key spatial/contextual details, yielding limited practical utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 3 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles and misclassified key contextual elements, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes like action and age but omits important semantics such as jaywalking and vehicle motion for moving vehicles, reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Places pedestrians on the sidewalk while ground truth indicates they are on the road/jaywalking, showing poor spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Partial correct attribute extraction is overshadowed by missed objects, incorrect scene context, and wrong spatial relations, leading to low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 5 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while missing two pedestrians and two moving vehicles, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attribute fields (actions, age, transport) but missed critical labels like jaywalking and misclassified one walking as standing, indicating moderate richness with errors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations but they cannot be fully validated against the ground truth and some pedestrian placements conflict with GT actions, so partial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Moderate extraction with reasonable attribute detail but notable omissions and at least one misclassification leading to limited reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 7 annotated objects (3/4 persons and 1/3 vehicles), missing one pedestrian and two moving vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Correctly labeled atomic actions and ages for pedestrians and identified a parked bicycle, but misclassified pedestrian simple_contexts (sidewalk vs road/jaywalking) and missed motion info for two vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial cues but they conflict with the ground-truth simple_context (claims sidewalk placement while ground-truth indicates on-road/jaywalking), making spatial relations unreliable.
justifications.overall: Partial detection and attribute extraction with correct walking actions but notable errors in pedestrian context and missing moving vehicles, yielding mediocre overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses two of four ground-truth pedestrians and one of two ground-truth vehicles, so it is substantially incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple semantic attributes (actions, age, transporting, communicative) beyond the ground truth but mislabels scene context for some pedestrians and omits two persons, reducing accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for the detected agents but are sparse and partly inaccurate relative to the ground truth since two pedestrians on the road were not represented.
justifications.overall: Due to missing objects and some misassigned contexts the extraction is of limited overall quality despite reasonable attribute coverage for detected entities.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only identifies 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and 2 of 3 ground-truth vehicles, missing several people and a moving vehicle, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the objects it does detect the VLM supplies many relevant attributes (actions, context, age, motion status, type, door/trunk states) that align with the ground-truth schema, though some attribute correctness is uncertain.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides plausible spatial relations for detected entities but these relations cover only a subset of ground-truth objects and include items that are not independently verifiable from the annotations.
justifications.overall: The output is semantically informative for detected objects but overall performance is limited by poor object-level recall, yielding a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 2 of 4 ground-truth pedestrians and 1 of 2 ground-truth vehicles, missing several objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Detected objects include multiple attribute fields (actions, context, age, motion, doors) providing reasonable semantics despite some generic labels and missing objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides plausible spatial relations for the reported agents (left shop, middle of road, ahead of ego) but is incomplete and somewhat imprecise for omitted agents.
justifications.overall: Moderate semantic detail for reported objects is offset by missed detections and incomplete coverage, yielding an overall middling score.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 7 pedestrians and missed the moving vehicle, so many ground-truth actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes like atomic_action, simple_context, and age are provided for detected people and generally match, but many actors and vehicle attributes are absent reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided vague sidewalk relations for detected people but failed to identify pedestrians on the road and a jaywalking case, leading to incorrect spatial coverage.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some correct attributes for a subset of pedestrians but misses most actors and the vehicle, resulting in limited overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 6 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles, so coverage is roughly half with several missed actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected actors the model provides many correct semantic labels (actions, age, vehicle motion and door/trunk states), but it missed multiple persons and some contextual details like pedestrians walking on the road.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial relations given are plausible for the listed objects (near left/right buildings, on the road) but several road-walking pedestrians were omitted and some spatial placement is incomplete.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: accurate attribute labeling for detected objects but significant omissions in detection reduce overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 8 pedestrians and the single vehicle, covering 4 of 9 ground truth entities so recall is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attributes (atomic actions, simple context, age, vehicle properties) for detected agents but missed or mislabelled key semantic detail such as jaywalking and omitted attributes for the many missed pedestrians.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for the detected agents and the vehicle and are mostly plausible, but relations are absent for most ground truth pedestrians and one crossing pedestrian is incorrectly described as using a pedestrian crossing.
justifications.overall: The extraction is accurate and rich for a subset of entities but suffers from low entity recall and some semantic misclassifications, yielding a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses all pedestrians and several vehicles present in the ground truth, capturing only two of five vehicles so it is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the VLM reports vehicle type, motion status, trunk and door states and a track hint, it omits pedestrian actions and important vehicle statuses such as the stopped vehicle, reducing semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: The model provides a correct left-side parking relation for the two extracted cars but fails to characterize spatial relations for other vehicles and for pedestrians, limiting usefulness.
justifications.overall: Due to missing pedestrians and multiple vehicles plus incomplete contextual labels, the extraction has limited reliability despite some correct vehicle attribute reporting.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses one pedestrian (the bicyclist/sitting person) and several vehicles from the five present, providing only a subset of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes some attributes like motion status, trunk and doors and age but omits critical contextual labels such as jaywalking and the bicyclist context and lacks object identifiers.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations exist for a couple of entities (parked along the left, near construction barriers) but are sparse and fail to describe relations for most vehicles and the jaywalking pedestrian.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a few correct attributes for some entities but is incomplete and misses key actors and contextual semantics, limiting its utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: VLM failed to detect any of the two persons and both vehicles present in the ground truth, so object-level completeness is zero.
justifications.semantic_richness: Only a high-level scene description was provided while all detailed semantic attributes (actions, ages, motion status) for annotated entities were omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations or positions between objects or relative to the road were reported despite ground truth containing 'walking on the road' and vehicle states.
justifications.overall: Given the complete omission of annotated persons and vehicles and only a minimal scene caption, overall performance is very poor.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses both pedestrians and two of the three vehicles from the ground truth and even incorrectly states there are no moving vehicles or pedestrians.
justifications.semantic_richness: Only a single vehicle with basic attributes is reported while actor actions, multiple vehicle motion statuses, and pedestrian behaviors are omitted and the scene description is incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: The left-side parking relation for one car appears correct, but spatial relations for other vehicles and pedestrians are entirely missing and no finer relations are provided.
justifications.overall: The extraction is sparse and partly incorrect, capturing one parked vehicle but failing to report multiple agents and mischaracterizing pedestrian and moving vehicle presence.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses one of the two pedestrians and two of the four vehicles and fails to report key contexts like jaywalking and a biking person, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, age, door/trunk states) but mislabels or omits important semantic details such as jaywalking and the second person activity, limiting its richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided (left side, behind, between buildings) and plausible, but relations are missing for multiple vehicles and the critical jaywalking spatial context is not captured.
justifications.overall: The output contains useful attributes and some correct relations but is incomplete and misses important actors and contexts, resulting in a below acceptable overall depiction of the scene.

justifications.completeness: Detected both people but misclassified one person and omitted most vehicles from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, context, age, spatial relations) showing richness, but several key attributes are incorrect or inconsistent with the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for each detected object, but they conflict with annotated contexts (sidewalk vs road) and many scene objects lack spatial descriptions.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat detailed extraction but notable inaccuracies in actions and contexts plus missing vehicles lead to limited usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Misses all pedestrians and most vehicles, detecting only one parked motorcycle while ground truth contains two pedestrians and four vehicles including a moving vehicle
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like type, motion_status, and trunk/doors state but omits actor actions, detailed person attributes, and motion labels for most vehicles present in the ground truth
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a plausible spatial relation for the detected motorcycle but fails to capture spatial relations for pedestrians and other vehicles and contradicts the scene claim of no pedestrians
justifications.overall: Significant omissions and at least one clear contradiction with ground truth yield low overall reliability despite a few correct attributes

justifications.completeness: Partial: the extraction captures the cyclist and pedestrian attributes but omits multiple parked vehicles from the ground truth and does not provide object ids.
justifications.semantic_richness: Moderate: the extraction provides several attributes (type, motion, context, age) but mislabels the pedestrian atomic_action, erroneously fills trunk and doors states for a bicycle, and lacks some vehicle attribute omissions present in ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Good: the supplied relations such as on the road and ahead of the ego vehicle are plausible and informative, though the ground truth contains no spatial tags for full verification.
justifications.overall: Below average: correct in key aspects like identifying a moving cyclist and its simple context, but missing multiple vehicles and containing notable attribute errors that reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses the bicyclist (counts the bike as a vehicle separate from its rider) and several ground-truth vehicles, providing only one pedestrian and one bicycle so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes some attributes like age and atomic action but mislabels the biking person and omits many vehicle attributes from the ground truth, yielding moderate-to-low semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides basic correct relations ('on the road', 'near the building on the right') for detected entities but misses spatial relations for multiple ground-truth objects, so coverage is partial.
justifications.overall: Captures a few correct elements but misclassifies actions, omits several objects and attributes, and thus has low overall accuracy and usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected the single person and a moving bicycle and a parked motorcycle but missed multiple other parked vehicles and misclassified the person's atomic action.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several semantic attributes (motion, trunk/doors, age, context) but fails to identify the person's atomic action and omits several vehicle instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and plausible (center of road, on road, near sidewalk) and align with the scene description.
justifications.overall: Extraction is moderately useful with good spatial and attribute reporting but incomplete instance coverage and an incorrect atomic action reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected the single person but only two of six ground-truth vehicles, missing most parked vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes like type, motion, and age but misses or mislabels key attributes (e.g., atomic action) and omits many vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for detected objects but omits relations for most ground-truth vehicles and may misplace the pedestrian relative to the sidewalk.
justifications.overall: Partial coverage with reasonable attribute detail for detected objects but significant omissions of vehicles and an incorrect atomic action reduce overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures a subset of objects (one pedestrian and three bicycles) but misses multiple ground-truth vehicles and mislabels the pedestrian atomic action, so many GT annotations are omitted or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides several attribute fields (type, motion_status, trunk/doors, age, spatial relations) adding useful detail, but some attributes are incorrect or unsupported by GT (atomic_action set to none) and object counts are incomplete.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and align with the scene description (cyclist on the left, bicycles parked on the right), showing strong localization despite other errors.
justifications.overall: While the extraction is semantically informative and spatially accurate, significant omissions and at least one incorrect action label reduce its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses two ground-truth vehicles, duplicates the bicycle as both pedestrian and vehicle, and fails to identify the pedestrian's atomic action ('sitting').
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes (motion, trunk/doors, age, contexts) but omits or mislabels key semantic details (atomic action, some vehicle presences) and adds possibly incorrect closed/open values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial placements (left, right, center) for the detected agents are generally plausible and align with the scene description, though ground-truth spatial labels are limited for full verification.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a useful subset of objects and attributes with reasonable spatial cues but misses multiple vehicles, misreports atomic action, and duplicates the bicycle, reducing reliability.

justifications.completeness: Only 4 of 7 ground-truth pedestrians were reported and all vehicles were omitted, with several transporting/contextual attributes missing, so major elements are absent.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes basic attributes (atomic action, age, some spatial tags and one communicative act) but misses multiple transporting labels and several contextual details present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some left/right sidewalk and directional cues are given, but notable spatial relations are incorrect or incomplete (e.g., a crossing pedestrian labeled as on sidewalk) and vehicle positions are not provided.
justifications.overall: The output captures a few correct atomic actions and one communicative act but suffers from omissions of multiple pedestrians, transport actions, contextual labels and all vehicles, limiting its overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only 4 pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth lists 7 persons and 1 vehicle, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes (age, atomic_action) are provided but many important labels (transporting/pushing, carrying, communicative cues, jaywalking) are omitted or incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided high-level spatial cues (middle of intersection, near sidewalk) are partially useful but many relative positions and lane-crossing/jaywalking contexts from GT are missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a few correct labels and basic spatial notes but misses the majority of actors, vehicle presence, and several key semantic details, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 pedestrians and no vehicles whereas ground truth contains eight pedestrians and three vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes like transporting and communicative signals are largely omitted or incorrect and vehicle attributes are missing, limiting semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided and match a few ground-truth labels but key relations like jaywalking and vehicle proximities are missing or misidentified.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a subset of pedestrians and some spatial info but omits many objects, transport states, and vehicle data, yielding limited overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth lists six persons and two vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes like age and atomic actions but misses key labels such as transporting for multiple pedestrians and a communicative state, and introduces an unsupported standing traffic officer label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Captures some relations like on the road and near barriers but fails to represent distinctions such as jaywalking versus walking along the side indicated in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction contains some correct basic detections and spatial hints but omits many actors and important semantic attributes, resulting in low overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Captured only 2 of 7 pedestrians and omitted all vehicles, missing the majority of annotated agents.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several semantic attributes per detected pedestrian (actions, context, transporting, age) but omits many annotated actors and some ground-truth attribute types, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes two spatial relations but they are sparse and partially mismatched to ground-truth positions, and no vehicle spatial relations are provided.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is incomplete and only partially accurate with moderate attribute detail but misses most agents and all vehicles, resulting in a low overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: Only four pedestrians were reported and no vehicles were detected despite the ground truth containing eight people and four vehicles, so many relevant objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple attribute fields but frequently uses none of the above, misses transporting annotations present in the ground truth, and omits vehicle semantics, limiting useful detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some relative placements are provided but they are coarse and partly inconsistent with ground truth simple context distinctions between on-road and sidewalk, giving partial correctness.
justifications.overall: The output captures a few actors with some attributes but omits many actors and several important semantic and spatial labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only three pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains seven persons and three vehicles, so many objects are missing.
